"'''Showdown'''" is a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Al Williamson]]. It was published by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]].

==Plot summary==
{{Plot}}

==Continuity==
[[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]] is mistakenly referred to as "[[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckass]]" in this story.

''Showdown'' is the 15th comic strip of the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' comic series]] written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] in the early 1980s (and later reprinted in [[Classic Star Wars 1|''Classic Star Wars'' 1]]-[[Classic Star Wars 20|20]] in the 1990s).

The series was written in a manner showing the stories unfold one after the other without interruption.<ref name="TCC" /> The novel ''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]'', which reused content from the comic strips, also presented a direct continuity between the stories.<ref name="ANHTLoLS">''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]''</ref> However, [[Lucasfilm]] contested this chronology because the existence of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] at the time of the [[Evacuation of Yavin]] (in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]) was in contradiction with other ''Star Wars'' sources. Lucasfilm therefore introduced a gap of several years between "[[Race for Survival]]" and "[[The Paradise Detour]]" (between [[Classic Star Wars 16|''Classic Star Wars'' 16]] and [[Classic Star Wars 17|17]]).<ref name="TCC" /> Accordingly, [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] dated ''Classic Star Wars'' 17-20 to [[2 ABY/Legends|2]]-[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="TEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> and other ''Star Wars'' material placed the death of [[Skorr/Legends|Skorr]] and the last events of the series in 3 ABY.<ref name="TCC" /><ref name="TEGtPaM" /><ref name="TEGtC" /><ref name="TESB" /><ref name="DB" />
